title: Lesson 5 Overview
subtitle: Listen to the lesson explained in English

transcript.en: Right! 👋 Lesson 5. Three minutes. Big payoff. Let's go.
Two big things today. Fruit and veg... and a magic trick that turns Maltese verbs ON. ✨
**Every Maltese fruit comes in THREE forms.** Not three colours — three forms.
ONE of the thing 🍏 • the thing IN GENERAL 🍎 • a SPECIFIC FEW 🍎🍎🍎
Watch. Apple. ONE apple in your hand:
Apples in general — like 'I love apples':
Just chop the **'a'** off the end. Done.
ONE word, three forms. Same shape **every time**.
Sixteen fruit, sixteen veg in this lesson — all the **same shape**. Don't try to memorise them today!
Forget the word 'imperative' for a second. Let's call it the **boss voice**.
It's what you use when you tell someone what to do. 'Sit!' 'Walk!' 'Read this!' That's the boss voice.
Two flavours: **one for one person, one for a group.**
Stick a **'u'** on the end → group command. **One rule.**
Once you've got the boss voice, you've basically already learned the **whole present tense**.
Same word. Stick a tiny letter on the **front**. That changes WHO is doing it.
Yep — same as 'you'. Maltese shares that one. ✂️
**N — T — J — T**. I, you, he, she. Plural? **Same letters again.** N-T-J. 🥁
Whole present tense: **N-T-J-T-N-T-J**. Seven forms. Three letters. 🎯
Fruit shapes. Boss voice. Magic letters. That's lesson 5.
Open a section. Tap. Listen. Repeat. You've got this. 💪
